item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | VERE’S TAKE | What’s in the budget for you?
item.description: Bula Vinaka shoppers, The hot topic in many households today is the rising cost of living. From the price of fuel to the cost of basic groceries, we are all feeling the pressure as we struggle to make ends meet at home. The other day, I went to fill up my car and realised just how much more I had to pay. What used to cost me $40 to top-up ended up costing $75. It was a stark reminder of how rising
item.content:encoded:
Bula Vinaka shoppers, The hot topic in many households today is the rising cost of living. From the price of fuel to the cost of basic groceries, we are all feeling the pressure as we struggle to make ends meet at home.

The other day, I went to fill up my car and realised just how much more I had to pay. What used to cost me $40 to top-up ended up costing $75. It was a stark reminder of how rising fuel prices is affecting everyone. A trip to the market in downtown Suva tells a similar story.

A tray of eggs now costs up to $20, and the prices of many essential items keeps creeping up week after week. Last Friday, I noticed something unusual at a supermarket that is normally packed around 7pm.

A cashier told me customers were more cautious with their spending and only buying what they needed.

Arun from Dilkusha raised concerns over the high cost of imported fruits and vegetables, pointing to avocados at $36 per kilogram, capsicums at $24.99/kg, raspberries at $127.60/kg, mandarins at $25/kg, tomatoes at $29/kg, broccoli at $31/kg and celery at $8.99/kg. She said similar produce was cheaper in Australia and New Zealand, citing celery at 79 cents a head and zucchini at 69 cents a kilogram. She believes supermarkets need to negotiate better deals with suppliers and lower their profit margins to make these products more affordable. Red onions and red cabbages have also become unaffordable.

Most families are cutting back, changing their shopping habits and finding new ways to cope with increases. Against this backdrop, all eyes are on the 2026–2027 National Budget, delivered in Parliament today.

There is hope that it will provide some relief at a time when household finances are under significant strain.

The Government has indicated the budget will focus on easing the cost of living, support businesses and economic stability, maintain critical public services and ensure fiscal discipline while introducing reforms for growth. The budget, we hope will bring relief to the low to mid income earners.

This as we continues to face economic pressures from rising fuel prices, increasing public debt, inflation concerns and uncertainty in the global economy. Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka says concerns raised during the recent State of the Fijian Economy Dialogue were considered as the Government finalised the budget, although details remain confidential until the official announcement today.

For ordinary citizens like Kata, the biggest hope is simple that the budget will bring lower prices for goods and services to allow him to spend less when buying food and paying for services.

However, he was quick to acknowledge that immediate reductions may not be realistic given the wider economic challenges.

Vili shares a more sceptical view, saying it is difficult to expect positive change when leaders do not feel the hardships faced by the grassroots people as they earn high salaries.

He said parliamentarians were paid by poor taxpayers funds and drove in posh vehicles without paying anything.

These opinions reflect the expectations and frustrations of many families.

However, it’s still important to understand that a national budget is much more than a list of taxes, spending plans and government announcements.

It is one of the Government’s most important tools for managing the economy and responding to the needs of its people. It certainly determines how much money is collected and where it will be spent.

The choices made influence almost every aspect of daily life, from healthcare and education to roads, infrastructure, social welfare, agriculture, job creation and support for businesses. A good budget must strike a careful balance.

It needs to provide relief for families struggling today while also managing government debt and investing in Fiji’s long-term development.

This balance is especially important for Fiji as a small island nation vulnerable to global fuel and food price shocks, climate-related disasters and changes in key sectors such as tourism.

The budget must therefore not only address the challenges of today but also prepare the country for the uncertainties of tomorrow.

As Fijians listen to today’s budget announcement, the question on many peole’s minds is simple: Will it make life a little easier for ordinary families feeling the pinch of the war and the rising costs of fuel and food?

Everyone wants to spend less to make life more manageable in these challenging economic times.

Let’s wait and see what our Government has to offer us!

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | The floor beneath your feet | The quiet power of good tiles at Vinod Patel
item.description: There is a building material so common we walk over it every day without a second thought, yet so important that its absence is felt immediately. Tiles have been used to define the character of a space for thousands of years from the mosaic floors of ancient Rome to the clean porcelain bathrooms of modern Fiji. They are functional, beautiful, and when chosen well, they are the single detail that t
item.content:encoded:
There is a building material so common we walk over it every day without a second thought, yet so important that its absence is felt immediately. Tiles have been used to define the character of a space for thousands of years from the mosaic floors of ancient Rome to the clean porcelain bathrooms of modern Fiji. They are functional, beautiful, and when chosen well, they are the single detail that ties an entire room together.

A tile does more than cover a surface. It manages moisture, repels bacteria, withstands heat, and survives decades of foot traffic without complaint.

In Fiji’s tropical climate, where humidity is high and the sun beats hard, the right floor tile keeps surfaces cool underfoot and prevents the moisture damage that destroys timber and carpet over time. A ceramic or porcelain tile laid correctly is arguably the most durable flooring choice available to a Fijian homeowner.

The right tile transforms a house into a home — and lasts a lifetime.

Wall tiles go further still. In bathrooms and kitchens, they create a fully waterproof surface that can be wiped clean in seconds eliminating the mould problems that plague painted walls in wet areas. In living spaces and outdoor entertainment areas, a well-chosen wall tile adds texture, depth, and architectural character that paint simply cannot achieve. The Nanhai Porcelain Matt Rustic range, for instance, brings the raw texture of natural stone to any wall at a fraction of the cost of quarried material.

The shift to 600×600 and larger format tiles in recent years has transformed interior design across the Pacific. Large format tiles create the illusion of more space fewer grout lines mean the eye travels further before stopping, making even a modest room feel expansive and modern.

The Lezora 600×600 Porcelain Gloss range achieves this effect beautifully, its reflective surface bouncing light around the room and amplifying natural brightness.

From the entry porch to the master bathroom, from outdoor patios to commercial shop floors, tiles are the foundation on which the character of a building is built. Vinod Patel’s Mega Tiles Sale running from 1st June to 19th July 2026 brings Fiji’s widest range of floor and wall tiles to you at up to 50% off regular price. Whether you are building new, renovating, or simply refreshing a tired room, there has never been a better time to tile.

For many Fijian families, the gap between dreaming of a beautifully tiled home and actually having one comes down to a single word: cash. Large tiling projects require purchasing by the carton, and the upfront cost of tiling a bathroom, kitchen, and living area can easily run into several thousand dollars.

That financial hurdle has historically pushed renovation and building projects back by months or years projects started when money is available rather than when they should be done.

Vinod Patel’s Hire Purchase programme changes that equation entirely. Through a simple online application, customers can purchase tiles and any other home and building product and spread the full cost over 36 months with zero deposit required. There are no large upfront payments, no need to drain savings, and no delays waiting for funds to accumulate.

You choose your tiles today, they go home with you today, and you pay in comfortable monthly instalments that fit within a regular household budget.

$0 deposit. 36 months. Fast approvals. Your tiles go home today.

The application process is fast and straightforward. Customers apply online through vinodpatel.com.fj, Fiji’s only dedicated home and building online store, and receive a response quickly often the same day. Approval requires minimal documentation, and once approved, the full purchase value is available immediately. There is no need to visit a bank or complete complex paperwork. The entire process is designed to remove friction and get your project moving.

Shop Anywhere, Any Time www.vinodpatel.com.fj

Fiji’s homes are being built and renovated from every corner of the archipelago. A builder in Labasa, a homeowner in Savusavu, a contractor finishing a resort fit out in Taveuni all of them need the same access to quality building materials that has historically been available only to those close to a major branch. Vinod Patel’s online store at vinodpatel.com.fj changes that permanently.

Launched as Fiji’s only dedicated home and building online store, vinodpatel.com.fj brings the full Vinod Patel product range — including the entire Mega Tiles Sale catalogue to any device with an internet connection. Browse tiles by category, size, finish, or price.

Check availability. And place your order without leaving your home, office, or worksite.

Critically, the Hire Purchase application is available directly through the website. Customers browsing tiles online can proceed from product selection to HP application in the same session without visiting a branch, without printing forms, and without waiting.

The digital HP process is designed to mirror the speed and simplicity of the in-store experience, bringing the same fast-approval finance to customers wherever they are in Fiji.

The Mega tiles sale is on now. up to 50% off. shop in store or online at vinodpatel.com.fj. apply for 36-month Hire Purchase with $0 deposit. while stocks last.

Tiles are available in a wide range of colors.

Stylish and durable bathroom tiles for every home.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Made of ingredients that transform the skin
item.description: VASELINE Gluta Hya 70x is one of most advanced skincare offered by Vaseline. Designed to deliver powerful brightening and hydration benefits, it is part of the Healthy Bright Gluta Hya Serum Burst range and is formulated to tackle dullness, uneven skin tone, and dryness. A standout feature is its GlutaGlow™ technology, which is 70x times more powerful than Vitamin C in fighting dullness and restor
item.content:encoded:
VASELINE Gluta Hya 70x is one of most advanced skincare offered by Vaseline. Designed to deliver powerful brightening and hydration benefits, it is part of the Healthy Bright Gluta Hya Serum Burst range and is formulated to tackle dullness, uneven skin tone, and dryness. A standout feature is its GlutaGlow™ technology, which is 70x times more powerful than Vitamin C in fighting dullness and restoring radiance. This makes it a compelling choice for individuals seeking visible improvements in skin clarity and brightness.

Vasleine Gluta Hya combines several key ingredients that work together to transform the skin. GlutaGlow™ technology targets dullness and its root, helping to reduce dark spots and improve overall skin tone.

Alongside this, the 4D Hyaluron complex provides multi-layer hydration by using hyaluron molecules of different sizes, ensuring that moisture penetrates deeply and keeps the skin supple throughout the day.

Another feature of Vaseline Gluta Hya 70x is its sun protection. With SPF50 Plus luminous protection, it shields your skin from harmful sun rays, preventing sun damage and premature aging. The lightweight serum burst formula absorbs quickly, leaving no greasy residue, which makes it convenient for everyday application.

The versatility of this product makes it suitable for various occasions. It can be used as daily sun protection, as post-sun care to restore hydration, or as part of an evening routine to nourish and repair your skin overnight. Its lightweight texture ensures that it fits seamlessly into any skincare regimen without feeling heavy or sticky.

Using Vaseline Gluta Hya 70x is simple. It is recommended to apply twice daily, massaging thoroughly onto the body or face to ensure even coverage. Consistency is key, as results are visible with regular use rather overnight.

It can also be used for both morning and evening routines, while also as a sun protection for the Luminous Defense range.

A product of Unilever, Vaseline is distributed in Fiji by Motibhai Group, sharing quality, sharing happiness.

Visit the Vaseline booth at the 2026 Fiji Showcase for exclusive deals.

Vasleine Gluta Hya combines several key ingredients that work together to transform the skin.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Wash your hands before eating
item.description: IN a world where people are increasingly aware of health and wellness, one of the simplest and most effective ways to protect ourselves from illness is often overlooked — washing our hands before eating. It is a habit most of us were taught as children, yet many people still forget to do it consistently.
item.content:encoded:
IN a world where people are increasingly aware of health and wellness, one of the simplest and most effective ways to protect ourselves from illness is often overlooked — washing our hands before eating.

It is a habit most of us were taught as children, yet many people still forget to do it consistently.

According to betterhealth.vic.gov.au, health experts continue to emphasise that proper handwashing with soap and water remains one of the most powerful tools in preventing the spread of infectious diseases and protecting families from harmful germs.

Every day, our hands come into contact with countless surfaces. From door handles and shopping trolleys to mobile phones, money, and public transport, germs can easily accumulate on our skin without us even noticing.

When we sit down for a meal without washing our hands, those germs can be transferred directly into our mouths, increasing the risk of illness.

Many infectious diseases are spread through contaminated hands. These include gastrointestinal illnesses that cause diarrhoea, vomiting, and stomach cramps, as well as respiratory infections such as colds, influenza, and other viruses.

For young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems, these infections can lead to serious health complications.

Washing hands before meals acts as a critical barrier against these diseases.

It removes bacteria, viruses, and other harmful microorganisms that may have been picked up throughout the day.

A simple 20-second handwash with soap and running water can significantly reduce the likelihood of infection.

The importance of hand hygiene became especially clear during the COVID-19 pandemic, when health authorities around the world encouraged regular handwashing as one of the primary methods of reducing the spread of the virus.

While the pandemic heightened awareness about cleanliness, the benefits of proper handwashing extend far beyond a single disease.

Good hand hygiene is particularly important in households with children. Young children frequently touch surfaces, play outdoors, and place their hands near their mouths.

Teaching them to wash their hands before meals helps establish lifelong healthy habits while reducing the risk of bringing germs into the home.

Parents play a crucial role in setting this example. When children see adults washing their hands before eating, they are more likely to follow the same behaviour.

Creating a routine around handwashing before breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks can make the practice a natural part of daily life.

Proper handwashing is straightforward but should be done correctly to be effective. Hands should be wet with clean running water before applying soap.

The soap should be worked into a lather and rubbed over all parts of the hands, including the backs of the hands, between the fingers, under the fingernails, and around the wrists.

This process should take at least 20 seconds before rinsing thoroughly under running water and drying with a clean towel or air dryer.

Many people rely on hand sanitiser when soap and water are not available. Alcohol-based hand sanitisers containing at least 60 per cent alcohol can help reduce germs in certain situations.

However, they are not always effective against all types of germs and cannot remove dirt or grease from the hands.

For this reason, health experts agree that washing with soap and water remains the gold standard, particularly before meals.

Hand hygiene also goes beyond protecting individual health.

It helps reduce the spread of illness within schools, workplaces, communities, and households.

Fewer infections mean fewer missed school days, reduced healthcare costs, and healthier communities overall.

For people living in rural areas or large family households, maintaining good hygiene practices can be especially valuable.

A single illness can quickly spread among family members, causing disruption and financial strain. Something as simple as washing hands before eating can help prevent such outbreaks and keep families healthy.

Looking after the skin on our hands is equally important. Frequent washing can sometimes cause dryness, so using a moisturising hand cream and protective gloves for household chores can help maintain healthy skin while continuing good hygiene practices.

At its core, handwashing is a small action with a significant impact. It requires only soap, water, and a few seconds of our time, yet it can prevent illness, protect vulnerable family members, and improve overall wellbeing.

Before every meal, taking the time to wash your hands is more than just a routine. It is an investment in your health and the health of those around you.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Refrigerators for every home and lifestyle
item.description: A refrigerator is no longer just an appliance it is the heart of the modern kitchen, helping families preserve food, reduce waste, and enjoy greater convenience every day. Today’s refrigeration technology offers a wide range of options designed to suit different household sizes, lifestyles, and budgets, with leading brands such as Haier, Fisher & Paykel, LG, and Hisense delivering innovative solut
item.content:encoded:
A refrigerator is no longer just an appliance it is the heart of the modern kitchen, helping families preserve food, reduce waste, and enjoy greater convenience every day.

Today’s refrigeration technology offers a wide range of options designed to suit different household sizes, lifestyles, and budgets, with leading brands such as Haier, Fisher & Paykel, LG, and Hisense delivering innovative solutions for every home.

For smaller households or those seeking reliable everyday performance, models such as the Haier 197L Top Mount Refrigerator and LG’s 266L and 315L Top Mount Refrigerators provide practical storage, efficient cooling, and strong energy performance.

Features including humidity-controlled crispers, spill-proof glass shelves, Door Cooling+™, and Multi Air Flow systems help keep food fresher for longer while maintaining low energy consumption.

Growing families can benefit from larger bottom mount and top mount designs such as the Haier 230L Bottom Mount, LG 420L Bottom Mount, and Hisense 292L Bottom Mount refrigerators. These models offer enhanced freezer capacity, convenient storage layouts, inverter technology, and frost-free operation, delivering both efficiency and ease of use.

For households that require greater capacity and premium functionality, French door, quad door, and side-by-side refrigerators represent the next generation of food preservation.

Haier’s 575L Three Door Side-by-Side and 507L Quad Door Refrigerators feature innovative Switch Zone™ technology, allowing users to convert storage space between fridge and freezer modes depending on changing needs.

Multi-Zone Air cooling systems ensure consistent temperatures throughout the appliance, while water and ice dispensers add everyday convenience.

Premium offerings from Fisher & Paykel showcase elegant design paired with advanced food-care technology.

The 515L Bottom Mount and 511L French Door refrigerators feature ActiveSmart™ technology, which intelligently maintains ideal temperatures to keep food fresher for longer.

With energy ratings of up to 6 stars, these models also demonstrate how performance and sustainability can go hand in hand.

Hisense continues to impress with spacious designs such as the 541L Side-by-Side and 496L Top Mount refrigerators, offering features including non-plumbed water dispensers, electronic touch controls, multi-airflow cooling, and high energy efficiency ratings.

Whether you are furnishing your first home, upgrading your kitchen, or seeking advanced food storage solutions, today’s refrigerator range offers exceptional choice, innovative technology, and energy-saving performance designed to meet the needs of modern Fiji households.

These renowned refrigerators are exclusively distributed and supported by Courts, the authorized partner for sales and after-sales service.

Customers enjoy a 2-year supplier warranty on Fisher & Paykel, Haier & LG with the option to purchase extended warranty coverage for added peace of mind that your product is protected for a longer period.

With Courts Credit, you can take your item home today and pay later in instalments.

Courts is proudly celebrating 106 years of serving customers, and you’re invited to be part of the celebration!

As a special thank you for your continued support, every purchase of $50 or more on any product at Courts automatically enters you into the draw to WIN an unforgettable 2-night stay at the beautiful DoubleTree Resort by Hilton.

Whether you’re shopping for furniture, appliances, electronics, or home essentials, now is the perfect time to enjoy great value while giving yourself the chance to win an exciting getaway.

Visit your nearest Courts store today and celebrate 106 years of trusted service, quality products, and rewarding experiences.

Follow our Facebook page and Viber group for quick updates on OB roadshow locations and the latest red pen deals.

Inset: There is a wide range of Fisher & Paykel refridgerators available at Courts.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Growing your own food helps save money
item.description: As grocery prices continue to climb, more people are looking for practical ways to reduce their food bills. One option that is gaining popularity is growing food at home even in little spaces available.
item.content:encoded:
As grocery prices continue to climb, more people are looking for practical ways to reduce their food bills.

One option that is gaining popularity is growing food at home even in little spaces available.

While many assume gardening requires a large backyard, experts say even people living in apartments or homes with limited outdoor space can successfully grow herbs and vegetables.

With a few pots, some sunlight and a little patience, households can enjoy fresh produce while saving money and improving their wellbeing.

Even if you live in an apartment or are short on outdoor space, there are ways to grow your own food at home.

Planting herbs and vegetables on windowsills, indoors or on small balconies can be a cost-effective option, as well as benefit your wellbeing.

Here’s what the experts say are the best way to get started this winter according to reports on ABC’s Gardening/Lifestyle website.

What can you grow? The beauty about the plant world is if their basic requirements like light, water, and nutrients are met, herbs and vegies can grow almost anywhere, says Melbourne/Naarm-based horticulturalist Justin Calverley.

Justin Calverley says Asian greens and herbs are the easiest to grow for beginner gardeners.

While growing food indoors can be trickier due to varying amounts of light and different types of air, he says certain leaf vegetables and herbs are the easiest to raise.

“On a windowsill, if you keep it very simple, you could grow lettuces, because they have very small root systems that could fit in a baked bean tin,” he explains.

Other vegies and herbs Calverley recommends cultivating in an indoor environment are: Asian greens such as bok choy, coriander, parsley and dill.

Plants with small root systems like curly parsley are suited to growing in small spaces.

On a balcony or in other outdoor areas, winter vegetables like spinach, kale, peas, broccoli, tomatoes and cauliflowers are simple to grow in pots.

Longtime small space gardener Wendy Siu-Chew used to grow her own food on a rooftop balcony for almost a decade before moving to her new home.

She says growing plants suited to the “cut-and-come-again” harvesting method offer best returns.

Chew Lee says for beginner gardeners, greens and herbs offer the best bang for your buck.

Growing plants best suited to the “cut-and-come-again” harvesting method are a simple way to enjoy fresh food throughout the growing season without replanting, she explains.

“You plant one or two lettuces, and you just keep going back and pick leaves as you want and it keeps growing.”

Lettuce is an easy vegetable beginner gardeners can grow indoors and outdoors.

“Cut-and-come again” is a practice where you only harvest the outer mature leaves of a plant, like lettuce or dhania while not disturbing the new leaves and root systems.

Cultivating your own herbs and vegies comes with multiple benefits for you, the environment and its sustainability of says Xiaoqi Feng, professor of urban health and environment at the University of New South Wales.

Feng highlights the mental and physical health benefits of growing your own food.

Growing food in an indoor or outdoor environment provides a microdose of nature, especially in built-up urban areas.

It can also improve your wellbeing. “We see that even small interactions with nature, like tending to herbs on a windowsill, can have a measurable benefit for mental and physical health,” she says.

“Watching something grow gives you a visible progress and is rewarding, it helps you to reduce stress and anxiety because it helps you create a sense of calm and routine.”

Growing food can support intergenerational bonding and social interaction.

Growing your own food also provides opportunities for social interaction, intergenerational bonding and reduced food waste, according to Feng.

She says cooking with herbs, for example, often only requires you to use a small amount, whereas store-bought bunches are large and often end up being wasted. “When you grow your own, you only harvest what you need, and it can sustainably grow back.”

Lee started her first balcony garden with repurposed polystyrene fruit boxes.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | How to cut your grocery bill
item.description: Experts say many people shop out of habit, which can make it harder to save money. Changing shopping routines, even temporarily, can help reduce costs, especially during the crisis we are going through with rising costs to food and fuel.
item.content:encoded:
Experts say many people shop out of habit, which can make it harder to save money.

Changing shopping routines, even temporarily, can help reduce costs, especially during the crisis we are going through with rising costs to food and fuel.

With the rising cost of groceries putting pressure on household budgets, many families are looking for practical ways to make their money stretch further.

While food prices may be beyond consumers’ control, experts say simple changes in shopping habits, meal planning and reducing waste can make a noticeable difference to the weekly grocery bill. This is what the experts say:

Before shopping, take stock of your pantry, fridge and freezer and plan meals around existing items.

Experts in Australia say households waste thousands of dollars’ worth of food each year, so reducing waste is one of the easiest ways to save.

Planning meals and writing a shopping list helps avoid impulse purchases. Buying versatile ingredients, such as mince, frozen vegetables and legumes, can also stretch meals further.

Frequent supermarket visits can lead to unnecessary spending. Experts recommend doing fewer, larger shopping to avoid being tempted by extra purchases.

Know exactly how much you spend on groceries by reviewing your expenses over several weeks and setting a practical weekly limit.

Money-saving strategies include: Buying cheaper cuts of meat such as mince and chicken wings. Adding lentils and beans to meals.

Eating more seasonal vegetables. Extending meals over two days.

Choosing supermarket or lower-cost brands. Buying discounted products close to their expiry dates. Comparing unit prices rather than assuming larger or loose items are cheaper.

Experts say many families are struggling with rising food costs, and it is important to maintain a balance by allowing small treats that bring enjoyment while staying within budget.

In the face of rising living costs, smarter grocery shopping has become more important than ever.

By planning, reducing food waste, sticking to a budget and making thoughtful choices at the supermarket, families can better manage their spending.

While saving money on groceries may require changing long-standing habits, experts say small, consistent changes can add up over time which helps households ease financial pressure while still enjoying the foods they love.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Keep veges and herbs fresh longer
item.description: THERE’S nothing quite as frustrating as reaching into your refrigerator for fresh vegetables, only to discover that they have become wilted, soggy, or discoloured. Accoding to smegshop.sg, for busy households, keeping produce fresh can feel like a constant challenge, especially in warm and humid climates where fruits and vegetables tend to spoil more quickly.
item.content:encoded:
THERE’S nothing quite as frustrating as reaching into your refrigerator for fresh vegetables, only to discover that they have become wilted, soggy, or discoloured.

Accoding to smegshop.sg, for busy households, keeping produce fresh can feel like a constant challenge, especially in warm and humid climates where fruits and vegetables tend to spoil more quickly.

The good news is that with a few simple storage techniques, you can significantly extend the life of your fresh produce, reduce food waste, and get more value from every grocery trip.

Here are some practical tips to help keep your fruits, vegetables, and herbs fresh for longer.

While it may seem convenient to wash fruits and vegetables immediately after purchasing them, excess moisture can actually accelerate spoilage.

Water left on produce creates the perfect environment for bacteria and mould to grow, causing vegetables to wilt and rot faster.

Instead, store produce unwashed and rinse it only when you are ready to use it. This is particularly important for leafy greens such as lettuce, spinach, and kale, which are highly sensitive to moisture.

2. Make use of your fridge’s crisper drawers

Most refrigerators are equipped with crisper drawers designed to regulate humidity levels. Using these compartments correctly can help preserve freshness for a longer period.

High-humidity drawers are best suited for vegetables that lose moisture quickly, such as leafy greens, carrots, broccoli, and herbs. The higher humidity prevents them from drying out and becoming limp.

Low-humidity drawers work best for fruits and vegetables that produce ethylene gas, including apples, pears, and avocados. Since ethylene can accelerate the ripening process of nearby produce, separating these items helps reduce spoilage.

Cutting fruits and vegetables ahead of time may save preparation time, but it significantly shortens their shelf life. Once produce is cut, its protective outer layer is removed, exposing it to air, moisture loss, and oxidation.

Whole fruits and vegetables retain their natural barriers, helping them stay fresh, crisp, and nutritious for much longer. Whenever possible, store produce whole and prepare it only when needed.

Plastic bags often trap moisture, creating condensation that encourages mould growth and spoilage. A better alternative is to use paper towels or breathable paper bags for storage.

For vegetables such as lettuce, herbs, broccoli, and celery, wrapping them loosely in paper towels helps absorb excess moisture while still allowing air circulation. This simple method can dramatically improve freshness and prevent sogginess.

Fresh herbs are among the most delicate items in the kitchen and can wilt within days if stored incorrectly.

To keep herbs fresh, treat them like a bouquet of flowers.

Trim the ends of the stems and place them upright in a glass or bottle filled with a small amount of water. Cover the herbs loosely with a plastic bag and store them in the refrigerator. This method works particularly well for parsley, cilantro, mint, and dill.

By keeping herbs hydrated, you can extend their freshness for up to two weeks, ensuring they remain vibrant and flavourful.

6. Maintain the correct fridge temperature

Temperature plays a major role in preserving food quality. For optimal freshness, keep your refrigerator between 1°C and 4°C.

At this temperature range, bacterial growth slows significantly while preventing delicate produce from freezing. If your fridge is too warm, fruits and vegetables will spoil faster. If it is too cold, some items may suffer from chilling damage, affecting their texture and flavour.

Regularly checking your refrigerator settings can help create the ideal environment for fresh produce.

A clean refrigerator is essential for maintaining fresh produce. Food spills, crumbs, and decaying leftovers can harbour bacteria and mould, which may spread to fruits and vegetables stored nearby.

Make it a habit to wipe shelves regularly, clean crisper drawers, and remove spoiled food promptly.

Maintaining a hygienic refrigerator helps reduce contamination and provides a healthier storage environment for your produce.

Sometimes it is impossible to use all your vegetables before they begin to spoil. Rather than throwing them away, consider freezing them.

Many vegetables freeze exceptionally well when blanched first. Blanching involves briefly boiling vegetables and then immediately cooling them in ice water. This process helps preserve their colour, texture, flavour, and nutritional value.

Once blanched, dry the vegetables thoroughly and store them in airtight freezer bags or containers. Frozen vegetables can last for several months and provide a convenient option for future meals.

How long do fruits, vegetables, and herbs last in the fridge?
